# fluent-sdfx

> A fluent, chainable Go API over [sdfx](https://github.com/snowbldr/sdfx), a signed-distance-function CAD kernel. Build 3D parts by composing primitives, transforms, and booleans on two immutable types: `*shape.Shape` (2D) and `*solid.Solid` (3D). All angles are in degrees, all constructors panic on invalid input.

This file is the agent-oriented reference for fluent-sdfx. If you take away one thing: **a fluent-sdfx program is a single expression that describes the part.** Every operation returns a new value, so chaining is the natural style — `solid.Cylinder(20, 10, 1).Cut(...).RotateZ(45).STL("out.stl", 5)`. Don't write a sequence of statements that mutate a builder; you'll be fighting the API.

## Mental model

- **Two core types.** `*shape.Shape` wraps `sdf.SDF2` (2D); `*solid.Solid` wraps `sdf.SDF3` (3D). Both are immutable: every method returns a new value. The receiver is never modified.
- **Degrees everywhere — with one exception.** All angle parameters are in degrees, converted to radians internally. The exceptions are `TwistExtrude`, `ScaleTwistExtrude`, and `InvoluteGearParms.PressureAngle`, which take **radians**. Use `units.DtoR(deg)` to convert.
- **Constructors panic on invalid input.** No `(*Shape, error)` return tuples. CAD-geometry errors are programming bugs, so you chain without `if err != nil` interruptions.
- **Z is up.** Cylinders, screws, sweeps, and the `RotateCopyZ` pattern all use Z as the axial direction — *except* `Revolve()` and `RevolveAngle()`, which revolve around the Y axis (an sdfx convention). For Z-axis revolutions, rotate the result 90° around X.
- **The 2D ↔ 3D direction.** `*shape.Shape` becomes `*solid.Solid` via `.Extrude`, `.Revolve`, `.LoftTo`, `.SweepHelix`, `.Screw`. `*solid.Solid` becomes `*shape.Shape` via `.SliceAt(plane.Plane)` (cross-section).
- **No build tool, no DSL.** Just Go. Each part is a `package main` with a `func main()` that calls `.STL(path, cellsPerMM)` at the end.

## Packages

- `solid/` — 3D primitives (`Box`, `Cylinder`, `Sphere`, `Cone`, `Capsule`, `Torus`, `Gyroid`), transforms, booleans, blends, patterns, modifiers.
- `shape/` — 2D primitives (`Rect`, `Circle`, `Polygon`, `Hexagon`, `Star`, `Cross`, `Triangle`, `Text`, `NewPoly()`, `NewBezier()`, threads, cams, gear racks), transforms, booleans, 2D→3D extrusion methods, `.ToDXF`, `.ToSVG`, `.ToPNG`.
- `obj/` — parametric helpers wrapping sdfx/obj (`Bolt`, `Nut`, `Washer3D`, `Panel3D`, `EuroRackPanel3D`, `PanelBox3D`, `Standoff3D`, `InvoluteGear`, `Servo3D`, `Spring3D`, `GfBase`/`GfBody` for Gridfinity, etc.). All take a parameter struct.
- `vec/{v2,v3,v2i,v3i,p2}` — vector types with named constructors: `v3.X(5)`, `v3.YZ(2, 3)`, `v3.XYZ(1, 2, 3)`, `v3.Zero`. `p2.RT(r, theta)` for polar.
- `plane/` — slice-plane helpers: `plane.AtZ(10)`, `plane.At(origin, normal)`, plus `plane.XY`, `plane.XZ`, `plane.YZ`.
- `units/` — constants and converters: `Pi`, `MillimetresPerInch`, `DtoR(deg)`, `RtoD(rad)`.
- `render/` — lower-level `ToSTL`, `To3MF`, `ToDXF`, `ToSVG`, `ToPNG` plus renderer constructors.
- `mesh/` — direct triangle-mesh utilities (bypass the SDF pipeline).

Install: `go get github.com/snowbldr/fluent-sdfx`.

## Constructor cheat-sheet

```go
// 3D primitives
solid.Box(v3.XYZ(20, 16, 10), 1.5)         // size, edge round
solid.Cylinder(20, 10, 1)                   // height, radius, edge round (axis: Z)
solid.Sphere(12)
solid.Cone(18, 10, 4, 0.5)                  // height, r0 (bottom), r1 (top), round
solid.Capsule(20, 6)                        // axial body length, radius
solid.Torus(12, 3)                          // major, minor

// 2D primitives
shape.Rect(v2.XY(30, 20), 3)                // size, corner round
shape.Circle(15)
shape.Polygon([]v2.Vec{...})
shape.Hexagon(12)
shape.Star(15, 7, 5)                        // outer, inner, points
shape.Text(font, "hello", 10)               // requires shape.LoadFont(path)

// Polygon / bezier builders
p := shape.NewPoly()
p.Add(0, 0)
p.Add(10, 0).Smooth(2, 6)                   // fillet this corner
p.Add(10, 5).Chamfer(1)
p.Close()
profile := p.Build()                         // returns *shape.Shape

// 2D → 3D
shape.Rect(...).Extrude(8)
shape.Rect(...).ExtrudeRounded(8, 1.5)       // top/bottom edge fillet
shape.Star(...).TwistExtrude(20, units.DtoR(90))   // twist in RADIANS
shape.Rect(...).ScaleExtrude(20, v2.XY(0.4, 0.4))  // tapered
profile.Revolve()                            // around Y axis
profile.RevolveAngle(270)                    // partial, in degrees
bottom.LoftTo(top, height, round)            // morph between profiles
profile.SweepHelix(radius, turns, height, flatEnds)
profile.Screw(height, start, pitch, num)     // num = parallel starts (1 = normal screw)

// Vectors
v3.XYZ(1, 2, 3)
v3.X(5)        // {5, 0, 0}
v2.XY(10, 20)
p2.RT(r, theta)  // theta in RADIANS
```

## Boolean cheat-sheet

```go
a.Union(b, c, d)                  // variadic; same as Add
a.Cut(tool1, tool2, tool3)        // variadic; same as Difference
a.Intersect(b)
solid.UnionAll(s1, s2, s3, ...)   // package-level when there's no obvious receiver

// Smooth variants (filleted/chamfered seams)
a.SmoothUnion(solid.RoundMin(2.5), b)         // RoundMin = circular fillet
a.SmoothUnion(solid.ChamferMin(2.5), b)       // ChamferMin = 45° chamfer
a.SmoothCut(solid.PolyMax(2.0), pocket)       // smooth-max for cuts/intersects
a.SmoothIntersect(solid.PolyMax(k), b)
```

Cut is **always** variadic in one call — `body.Cut(t1, t2, t3)` — never chain `.Cut(t1).Cut(t2).Cut(t3)`. The variadic form is faster (the SDF is sampled once) and cleaner.

## Transforms cheat-sheet

```go
// Translate
s.Translate(v3.XYZ(1, 2, 3))
s.TranslateX(5)
s.TranslateXY(2, 3)
s.TranslateXYZ(1, 2, 3)

// Rotate (DEGREES)
s.RotateX(90)
s.RotateZ(45)
s.RotateAxis(v3.XYZ(1, 1, 0).Normalize(), 30)
s.RotateToVector(v3.X(1), v3.Z(1))   // rotate so X axis aligns with Z

// Scale
s.Scale(v3.XYZ(1, 0.5, 1.5))         // per-axis
s.ScaleUniform(1 / 0.999)            // shrinkage compensation

// Mirror
s.MirrorXY()    // flip Z
s.MirrorXZ()    // flip Y
s.MirrorYZ()    // flip X

// Position helpers
s.Center()      // centre bounding box on origin
s.ZeroZ()       // move min-Z to 0 ("sitting on the build plate")
```

## Positioning cheat-sheet

The canonical way to place parts. Every solid has 27 named anchors on
its bounding box; align them to other anchors or to absolute
coordinates without writing bbox math by hand.

```go
import "github.com/snowbldr/fluent-sdfx/layout"

// Anchor selectors (return AnchoredSolid)
s.Top() s.Bottom() s.Right() s.Left() s.Back() s.Front()              // 6 faces
s.TopRight() s.BottomFront() s.FrontLeft() ...                         // 12 edges
s.TopFrontRight() s.BottomBackLeft() ...                               // 8 corners
s.AnchorAt(x, y, z int)                                                // arbitrary; each in {-1,0,+1}

// Relative placement (anchor → anchor) — returns Placement
cap.Bottom().On(body.Top()).Union()         // stack flush, then union
hole.Bottom().Above(body.Top(), 2).Cut()    // 2mm gap, then subtract
pocket.Top().On(body.Top()).Cut()           // align tops, then subtract
cap.OnTopOf(lantern.Top()).Union()          // sugar: cap above lantern's top, then union
m.Right().LeftOf(host.Left(), 1).Solid()    // returns *Solid (no boolean)
boss.OnTopOf(plate.Top()).SmoothUnion(solid.RoundMin(2)) // smooth fillet at the seam

// Sugar on *Solid (same chains, fewer anchors)
s.OnTopOf(t.Top())          // == s.Bottom().Above(t.Top())
s.UnderneathOf(t.Bottom())  // == s.Top().Below(t.Bottom())
s.RightOf(t.Right())        // == s.Left().RightOf(t.Right())
s.LeftOf(t.Left())          // == s.Right().LeftOf(t.Left())
s.InFrontOf(t.Front())      // == s.Back().InFrontOf(t.Front())
s.BehindOf(t.Back())        // == s.Front().Behind(t.Back())
s.Inside(t)                 // bbox center on bbox center

// Absolute (return *Solid directly)
s.AnchorAt(0,0,1).At(v3.XYZ(0, 0, 50))      // land an anchor on a literal point
s.Top().AtZ(50)                              // only Z; X/Y unchanged
s.BottomAt(0)  s.TopAt(50)
s.LeftAt(-20) s.RightAt(20)
s.FrontAt(-10) s.BackAt(10)
s.CenterAt(v3.XYZ(0, 0, 25))

// ShiftX/Y/Z move the anchor point without moving the solid yet
s.OnTopOf(plate.Top().ShiftY(-4))            // stack on top, but 4mm forward of center

// Placement finalizers — boolean partner is the target's owner
.Union()  .Add()                                  // unions the moved into the base
.Cut()    .Difference()                           // subtracts the moved from the base
.Intersect()                                      // intersect base with moved
.SmoothUnion(min)  .SmoothAdd(min)                // smooth-blended union with a MinFunc
.SmoothCut(max)    .SmoothDifference(max)         // smooth-blended cut with a MaxFunc
.SmoothIntersect(max)                             // smooth-blended intersect with a MaxFunc
.Solid()                                          // escape — moved part alone, no boolean

// 2D mirror (Shape) — same names, halved anchor set
s.Top() s.Bottom() s.Right() s.Left()
s.TopRight() s.BottomLeft() ...              // 4 corners
s.AnchorAt(x, y int)                         // 2D follows screen convention: +Y is up
```

### Layouts (positions for `.Multi(...)`)

```go
peg.Multi(layout.Polar(20, 6)...)            // 6 on a circle
peg.Multi(layout.PolarArc(20, 5, 0, 90)...)  // 5 spanning a 90° arc
hole.Multi(layout.Grid(10, 10, 4, 4)...)     // nx*ny grid centered on origin
post.Multi(layout.RectCorners(80, 50)...)    // 4 panel corners
peg.Multi(layout.BoxCorners(v3.XYZ(20, 10, 5))...) // 8 box corners
post.Multi(layout.Line(v3.X(0), v3.X(60), 4)...)   // n equally spaced
// 2D variants: layout.Polar2, layout.Grid2
```

### Reach-for guide

- `.Translate(v)` — literal vector, unrelated to other geometry.
- `anchor.At(p)` — land a specific edge/corner on a literal point.
- `anchor.On(target)` / sugar verbs (`OnTopOf`, ...) — relative to another part.
- `BottomAt(z)` / `LeftAt(x)` / ... — flush a face against an axis plane.
- `layout.X` + `.Multi(...)` — repeated copies in a regular pattern.

## Modifiers cheat-sheet

```go
s.Shell(thickness)           // hollow out — closed on all sides; Cut a face to open
s.Offset(distance)           // every surface along its normal (positive = outward)
s.Shrink(amount)             // uniform inset, faster than Offset
s.Grow(amount)               // uniform expansion (good for clearance fits)
s.Elongate(v3.X(10))         // insert a flat-walled extrusion between two halves
s.Cache()                    // pre-sample distance grid (huge speedup for text/complex polygons)
```

Shell is closed on every face. To make a *cup* or *open box*, Shell first then Cut away the side you want open: `solid.Cylinder(20, 12, 1).Shell(1.5).Cut(solid.Cylinder(4, 13, 0).TranslateZ(9))`.

## Patterns cheat-sheet

```go
s.Array(nx, ny, nz, v3.XYZ(stepX, stepY, stepZ))    // regular grid
s.RotateCopyZ(n)                                     // n copies around Z, evenly spaced
s.LineOf(p0, p1, "xxx.xxx")                          // along a line; 'x' = copy, '.' = skip
s.Multi([]v3.Vec{...})                               // explicit positions
s.Orient(base, []v3.Vec{...})                        // copies aligned to direction vectors

// Smooth-blended versions: SmoothArray, SmoothRotateUnionZ
```

`RotateCopyZ` orbits *the receiver as-is* around Z. Position the source off-axis (e.g. `.TranslateX(10)`) to get evenly-spaced copies; otherwise all copies overlap at the centre.

## Cross-sections (3D → 2D)

```go
import "github.com/snowbldr/fluent-sdfx/plane"

slice := shape.SliceAt(solid3d, plane.AtZ(0))   // returns *shape.Shape
slice.ToSVG("section.svg", 200)                 // export for laser cutting
slice.ToDXF("section.dxf", 200)
slice.Extrude(1).STL("section.stl", 8)          // re-extrude the slice for visualisation

plane.At(origin, normal)                         // arbitrary plane
```

## Output: cellsPerMM and decimation

```go
part.STL("part.stl", 5.0)            // mesh density in cells per real millimetre
part.STL("part.stl", 5.0, 0.5)       // optional decimation: keep 50% of triangles
part.ThreeMF("part.3mf", 5.0)        // 3MF (modern alternative; aliased as MF3)

shape.ToDXF(path, meshCells)         // 2D DXF
shape.ToSVG(path, meshCells)         // 2D SVG
shape.ToPNG(path, w, h)              // 2D PNG (renders the SDF as a heatmap, not a clean outline)
```

`cellsPerMM` is independent of part size — the same density gives the same visual quality on a 1mm gear and a 500mm enclosure. Render time scales as cells³, so halving density is an 8× speedup.

| Scenario | `cellsPerMM` |
|---|---|
| Iteration / preview | `1.0`–`2.0` |
| Final 50mm bracket | `5.0` |
| Final 10mm gear | `15`–`20` |

Tiny parts hit `solid.MinCells` (default 32) so a 1mm sphere at low density still produces a usable mesh.

## Parametric helpers (`obj` package)

Common helpers — all take a parameter struct, all panic on invalid input:

```go
obj.Bolt(obj.BoltParms{Thread: "M8x1.25", Style: "hex", TotalLength: 30, ShankLength: 6})
obj.Nut(obj.NutParms{Thread: "M8x1.25", Style: "hex", Tolerance: 0.1})
obj.Washer3D(obj.WasherParms{Thickness: 1.5, InnerRadius: 4.5, OuterRadius: 8.5})
obj.Panel3D(obj.PanelParms{
    Size: v2.XY(80, 50), CornerRadius: 4, HoleDiameter: 3.2,
    HoleMargin: [4]float64{6, 6, 6, 6},
    HolePattern: [4]string{"x.x", "x", "x.x", "x"},   // [top, right, bottom, left]
    Thickness: 3,
})
obj.Standoff3D(obj.StandoffParms{
    PillarHeight: 12, PillarDiameter: 6,
    HoleDepth: 8, HoleDiameter: 2.5,         // HoleDepth > 0 = tapped hole; < 0 = support stub
    NumberWebs: 4, WebHeight: 4, WebDiameter: 10, WebWidth: 1,
})
obj.InvoluteGear(obj.InvoluteGearParms{
    NumberTeeth: 24, Module: 2,              // pitch diameter = NumberTeeth × Module
    PressureAngle: units.DtoR(20),           // RADIANS — use DtoR
    Backlash: 0.05, Clearance: 0.2,
    RingWidth: 6, Facets: 16,
})
obj.GfBase(obj.GfBaseParms{Size: v2i.XY(2, 1), Magnet: true, Hole: true})  // Gridfinity
obj.PanelBox3D(obj.PanelBoxParms{...})       // returns []*solid.Solid
```

`Thread` strings are sdfx thread-table names: `"M8x1.25"`, `"M5x0.8"`, `"unc_1/4"`, `"unc_5/8"`, etc. Use `shape.ThreadLookup(name)` to inspect dimensions.

Other helpers in `obj`: `Hex2D/3D`, `HexHead3D`, `ChamferedCylinder`, `CounterBoredHole3D`, `ChamferedHole3D`, `CounterSunkHole3D`, `BoltCircle2D/3D`, `CircleGrille2D/3D`, `KeyedHole2D/3D`, `Keyway2D/3D`, `Knurl3D`, `KnurledHead3D`, `EuroRackPanel2D/3D`, `Pipe3D`, `StdPipe3D`, `Servo3D`, `ServoLookup`, `Spring2D/3D`, `Geneva2D` (returns driver and driven), `FlatFlankCam`, `ThreeArcCam`, `GearRack`, `Angle3D` (L-bracket), `TruncRectPyramid3D`, `Display`, `DroneMotorArm`, `DrainCover`, `Arrow3D`, `Axes3D` (debugging), `ImportSTL`, `ImportTriMesh`.

## Common idioms

- **Always make cutting tools longer/larger than the body.** A hole that's exactly as tall as the body leaves a single layer of mesh that may or may not survive marching cubes. Pad by 1–2mm.
- **`Cache()` text shapes** before further use. `shape.Text` re-samples glyph outlines at every distance evaluation; caching pays off as soon as you generate the geometry twice (e.g. once for DXF, once for STL).
- **Print-shrinkage compensation:** `part.ScaleUniform(1.0 / 0.999)` ≈ 0.1% (PLA). Apply at the very end, just before `STL`.
- **Mating gears must share `Module` and `PressureAngle`.** Centre distance is `(N1 + N2) × Module / 2`.
- **Variadic Cut is faster** than chained Cut. `body.Cut(t1, t2, t3)`, never `.Cut(t1).Cut(t2).Cut(t3)`.
- **Smooth blends propagate `k`.** A large `k` near a thin feature can deform that feature visibly. Match `k` to the smallest local feature you don't want disturbed.
- **For shells with an opening** (cup, box, tray), `s.Shell(t).Cut(openingTool)`. Shell on its own is closed on every face.

## A complete example

```go
package main

import (
    "github.com/snowbldr/fluent-sdfx/obj"
    "github.com/snowbldr/fluent-sdfx/solid"
    "github.com/snowbldr/fluent-sdfx/units"
    v3 "github.com/snowbldr/fluent-sdfx/vec/v3"
)

const shrink = 1.0 / 0.999 // PLA

func main() {
    gear := obj.InvoluteGear(obj.InvoluteGearParms{
        NumberTeeth: 24, Module: 2,
        PressureAngle: units.DtoR(20),
        Backlash: 0.05, Clearance: 0.2,
        RingWidth: 6, Facets: 16,
    }).ExtrudeRounded(4, 0.4)

    hub := solid.Cylinder(8, 8, 0.5).TranslateZ(2)
    shaft := solid.Cylinder(20, 3, 0)
    setScrew := solid.Cylinder(20, 1.25, 0).RotateY(90).TranslateZ(4)

    part := gear.Union(hub).Cut(shaft, setScrew).ScaleUniform(shrink)
    part.STL("gear.stl", 6.0, 0.5)
}
```

## Doc pages (deeper detail)

- [/install](/install/) — Go, fluent-sdfx, f3d.
- [/project-setup](/project-setup/) — scaffolding a Go module, picking a layout for assemblies.
- [/dev-loop](/dev-loop/) — `stldev` watch-rebuild-preview loop.
- [/quickstart](/quickstart/) — five-step incremental walkthrough.
- [/vectors-types](/vectors-types/) — v2, v3, p2, the conversion helpers.
- [/shapes-2d](/shapes-2d/) — every 2D primitive, the polygon and bezier builders.
- [/solids-3d](/solids-3d/) — every 3D primitive.
- [/booleans](/booleans/) — Union, Cut, Intersect.
- [/transforms](/transforms/) — Translate, Rotate, Scale, Mirror.
- [/positioning](/positioning/) — anchors (Top, BottomFrontRight, ...), placement verbs (On, Above, OnTopOf, Inside, BottomAt, ...), and `layout` helpers (Polar, Grid, RectCorners). The canonical way to place parts.
- [/2d-to-3d](/2d-to-3d/) — Extrude, Revolve, Loft, SweepHelix, Screw.
- [/smooth-blends](/smooth-blends/) — RoundMin, ChamferMin, PolyMax, etc.
- [/modifiers](/modifiers/) — Shell, Offset, Shrink, Grow, Elongate.
- [/patterns](/patterns/) — Array, RotateCopyZ, LineOf, Multi, Orient.
- [/cross-sections](/cross-sections/) — slicing 3D solids back to 2D.
- [/text-2d-output](/text-2d-output/) — Text rendering, DXF/SVG/PNG.
- [/obj-overview](/obj-overview/) — tour of the parametric helpers.
- [/output-resolution](/output-resolution/) — cellsPerMM, decimation, 3MF.
- [/cookbook-bolt](/cookbook-bolt/) — bolt + nut + washer assembly.
- [/cookbook-enclosure](/cookbook-enclosure/) — printable enclosure.
- [/cookbook-gear](/cookbook-gear/) — gear with hub, shaft bore, set-screw.
- [/cookbook-lantern](/cookbook-lantern/) — tea-light lantern showcasing the positioning verbs end-to-end.
- [/api-reference](/api-reference/) — flat reference of every type and method.
